title: A Sea of Green: Attica Metropolitan Parks
clientType: Region of Attica — metropolitan parks programme
summary: Microsite for the Region of Attica presenting A Sea of Green, its programme to regenerate and connect metropolitan parks across greater Athens. Designed in Figma, built in WordPress with Elementor.
problem: A green-space programme that runs across several parks, agencies, and years is hard to grasp from any single page. The microsite had to make the whole scope legible in one short visit, to residents and to a European audience, on any device, without assuming they already know the projects.

cover.caption: Home
cover.alt: Region of Attica microsite home page, an aerial view of Faliro Bay behind the heading A Sea of Green

gallery.caption: Metropolitan green network
gallery.alt: Green Areas page with a park photograph and the heading Growing a Metropolitan Green Network for Greater Athens

gallery.caption: Aenaon Park
gallery.alt: Aenaon Park page showing the park signage and its open Mediterranean park identity

gallery.caption: Pedion Areos
gallery.alt: Pedion Areos page with a photograph of the park's planted paths

sections.heading: Context
sections.body: The Region of Attica is building a network of metropolitan parks for greater Athens: the regeneration of Faliro Bay and the new Aenaon park on the coast, alongside established green spaces such as Pedion Areos, Antonis Tritsis, and Attiko Alsos. The microsite presents this work in Greek and English under one name, A Sea of Green, and carries the region's presence for the European Week of Regions and Cities in October 2025.

sections.heading: Challenge
sections.body: Each park sits at a different stage and has its own history, so a set of separate pages could easily read as unrelated projects. The design needed to tie them into a single programme, and to hold a register fit for a regional authority and a European audience while staying open to the residents who will use the parks.

sections.heading: Process
sections.body: I designed one page template and carried it across the programme. A rounded content panel overlaps an angular cut of a photograph, a large dark heading names each place, and a short blue subheading underneath gives its one-line promise before the detailed text. A triangular motif drawn from the Region of Attica mark runs through the pages as framing and dividers. The Aenaon park keeps its own signage and colour marks inside the shared system, so it reads as part of the whole while still standing on its own.

sections.heading: Output
sections.body: A bilingual microsite with a home overview and a dedicated page for each park. The home hero looks down over the coastline and sends visitors either to Aenaon or to the wider metropolitan parks. Interior pages open on a photograph of the specific park, then set out what it is, its scale, and the uses it is planned for.

sections.heading: Result
sections.body: The programme now has one public reference point. A resident, a reporter, or a European counterpart can open it and see what is being built across Attica's green spaces, and where, without piecing it together from separate sources.
